articles.title: US signs agreement for construction of permanent embassy in Jerusalem
articles.summary: US signs agreement for construction of permanent embassy in Jerusalem The US signed an agreement on Wednesday to build a new embassy compound in southern Jerusalem. US ambassador Mike Huckabee said during the signing ceremony at Israel’s foreign ministry that Washington “recognizes Jerusalem as the eternal, indigenous, and forever capital of the Jewish people”. “We are going to plant our flag, our American flag, on the soil of Jerusalem for a permanent and a brand new embassy compound that will serve as our mothership of diplomatic activities here in Israel,” he added. articles.summary: More than 704,000 Moroccans living abroad entered the country between June 10 and 30 under the Marhaba 2026 reception operation, up 3.04% from the same period last year, according to figures from the Mohammed V Foundation for Solidarity. Airports handled 417,611 arrivals, or 59.25% of the total, while maritime entry points received 287,103 travellers. articles.title: Tankmaker KNDS Postpones Its IPO Citing Market Conditions
articles.summary: Tankmaker KNDS intends to resume its initial public offering process when market conditions improve, according to a Wednesday statement, postponing what was set to be one of Europe’s largest debuts in recent years.

articles.title: New Zealand House Prices Near Three-Year Low on Iran War Worries
articles.summary: New Zealand house prices fell for the third straight month in June, nearing a three-year low and adding to signs of an economic slowdown as the global oil shock hits household incomes and consumer confidence.

articles.title: Headline: Telecom Shakeup: Comcast, Verizon Make Deals
articles.summary: Bloomberg's Michelle Davis joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Verizon and the UK’s BT Group agreed to create a joint venture for their international businesses in a merger that will take the low-margin units off their books and allow the carriers to focus on their home markets. Also, Comcast announced on Monday that it will spin off NBCUniversal and its European media unit, Sky, into a separate, independently traded company. Comcast will hang onto its cable-TV, broadband and wireless businesses. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: M&A Environment Healthy: Top M&A Firm

articles.title: Iranian president insists supreme leader did not bar negotiations
articles.summary: Iranian president insists supreme leader did not bar negotiations Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian defended Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei against allegations that he did not sign off on the memorandum of understanding with the US. Pezeshkian was quoted by Iran’s IRNA news agency as saying that if Khamanei “had ordered not to negotiate, we would have definitely obeyed”. This comes after controversy was sparked when the supreme leader said he “held a different view” on the agreement.

articles.title: Blackstone Offloads Stakes in Trio of Virginia Data Centers
articles.summary: Bloomberg's Anthony Hughes joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." Blackstone is selling its stakes in a trio of data centers across Northern Virginia for $3.5 billion, cashing out of part of a bet it made less than three years ago. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: USMCA Was a Great Success Story For North America Says Timmons
articles.summary: The US won’t renew its trade deal with Canada and Mexico, choosing instead to conduct annual reviews of the pact in a decision that risks adding uncertainty for companies producing goods across North America. Jay Timmons, president and CEO of the National Association of Manufacturers joined Balance of Power and said the US trade deal with Canada and Mexico was a "great success story." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Trump Reports at Least $1.4 Billion in 2025 Crypto Earnings

articles.title: Iran says talks in Qatar have concluded
articles.summary: Iran says talks in Qatar have concluded Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi announced on Wednesday that talks with Qatari and Pakistani officials in Doha on implementing the memorandum of understanding between the US and Iran have concluded.  The participants agreed that “a communication channel would be established by tomorrow” to report and record violations of the agreement, the IRNA news agency quoted Gharibabadi as saying. He added that it has been decided that part of the $6b in frozen funds would be used to purchase goods based on Iran’s needs.

The main gap Nato is struggling to fill has been its strategic bombers, after the US said it will only provide one aircraft rather than two, the source said, speaking on condition of anonymity.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill at least three in Gaza city
articles.summary: Israeli strikes kill at least three in Gaza city Wafa news agency reported two Israeli strikes that have left at least three dead and several critically injured in northern Gaza City on Wednesday. An Israeli combat drone struck near al-Helou Feil Station, killing three people, according to local sources. Several Palestinians were wounded in an earlier Israeli drone strike targeting a group of people near Dar al-Arqam School in the Sheikh Radwan neighbourhood. Eight Palestinians have been killed by Israel in the last 24 hours, and 26 others were admitted to Gaza hospitals, Wafa news reported.
